comments.text:
Sir mere papa ne home loan lia tha 30L ka + 30 L ka top up loan lia 2 months pehle 

But icici wale bol rahe hai ki ispar insurance ni  lia hai 
But mere papa  ka suddenly dehant kuch din pehle hua tha usse kuch din pehle papa ne  maa ko bola  tha   ki chinta mat karo maine loan ka insurance karwaya hai  mai agar chala jau to kisi ko bhi payment ni krna hoga  


Lekin bank ab deny kar raha hai 
Mere paas proof nahin hai 
Kaise pata karu ki unhonne  loan lia tha ya nahin ,ya fir form par sign kia tha ya nahin loan k liye iska pata kaise lagau

comments.text: Aapko Loan ka statement nikalwana hoga...waha par disbursement ke sath insurance ki money bhi deducted hue hogi. agar hue hai tto aapka insurance hua hai agar nahi hue tto aapka insurance nahi hua hai...

comments.text:
Hi sir please reply back,
Waiting for your answer!!😢
We bought a 2nd hand house of G+2 floors in Hyderabad 25 days ago which is 30 years old building and we got a insurance in ICICI on both structure and home loan insurance ,but when we were renovating the house with new windows unfortunately whole building collapsed (completely) i really want to know what can I do know ,can i clam a loan insurance now if yes what amount will i get (percentage) 
Plz reply sir iam waiting 😢
comments.author: @thekrazythinkers5179

comments.text: You can claim but we need to find out the reason of collapse. May be there is untheorized construction or something else.  The insurance surveyor will decide after that your claim will be pass. Moreover they will check Proposal form that all relevant information was correct or not.
comments.author: @InsurancewithSachin

comments.text: See it's too easy to get insurance but almost impossible to get claim

comments.text: Thanks for the really nice and detailed explanation. In case of any accident damaging the contents, will it need the invoices of those contents for getting the recovery amount.

comments.text: You should go for reinstated value rather then market value...market value charged depreciated value...

comments.text: As such NOT because they can access and evaluate via damaged contents too...If you have invoices then much better.
